title: Genital wart documentation
source_review_basis: full authoritative documents inspected and archived; item-level retain/rewrite/remove/add comparisons recorded

assessment.rationale: Exact phrase located in inspected full source.

source.title: Sexually Transmitted Infections Treatment Guidelines, 2021 — Genital Ulcers, Anogenital Warts, and Human Papillomavirus Infections
source.jurisdiction: United States federal public-health guidance requiring UAE epidemiology, laboratory, medicine and reporting adaptation
source.population: Sexually active adolescents and adults with genital, anal or perianal ulcers, anogenital warts, HPV infection or related counselling needs, with pregnancy, immune-status, anatomy and exposure qualifiers.
source.setting: Clinical STI evaluation, diagnosis discussion, counselling and clinician-selected management.
source.exclusions: Exact for recording lesion history and examination, differential context, clinician-selected testing, entered wart or HPV findings, partner or STI context and counselling. It does not diagnose an STI, infer sexual exposure, choose treatment or define UAE reporting pathways.

exact_location.heading: Anogenital warts — diagnostic considerations and counselling

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports clinician-observed wart morphology and site, differential diagnosis, biopsy context when indicated, immune and pregnancy context, and counselling without inferring HPV type or cancer.

assessment.text: Supports clinician-observed wart morphology and site, differential diagnosis, biopsy context when indicated, immune and pregnancy context, and counselling without inferring HPV type or cancer.
assessment.rationale: Supports clinician-observed morphology and qualified evaluation.

assessment.text: Supports documenting an entered HPV result, the limitations of source attribution and timing, partner discussion and screening-programme context; it does not diagnose cancer or identify the source of infection.
assessment.rationale: Supports accurate counselling without inferring infection source or timing.
